mechanism_narrative: A persistently near-zero federal funds rate (currently 0.08%) signals an accommodative monetary stance that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing a sustained upward drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the following month regardless of short-term fluctuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or mean forward return during low-rate periods is not significantly greater than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above 4.3% from the current 4.0% base, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that triggers rapid risk-off repricing in equities over the next trading week, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.3% (0.3pp increase from current 4.0% level)
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where the UNRATE threshold trigger fires, or Rank IC is not distinguishable from zero (|IC| < 0.02) conditional on trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in retail sales (RSAFS at 626,833M) and housing starts (HOUST at 1,702K units) indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion that compounds through the supply chain, driving above-trend corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter, but neither series alone is sufficient because retail strength without housing follow-through historically reverses within two months.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS-HOUST composite signal fails to outperform either single-series baseline in Rank IC by more than 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, indicating no genuine interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO at 101.89) reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization that feeds directly into corporate revenue growth, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trend growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive INDPRO growth periods do not show statistically higher forward returns than negative growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in CPIAUCSL beyond 0.4% signals renewed inflationary pressure that raises expected future policy tightening, causing an immediate risk-off reaction in equities within the following trading week and negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following triggered CPI acceleration events are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the conditional mean return is not significantly below baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening or restrictive-rate regime, the combination of decelerating PAYEMS growth alongside a rising UNRATE signals genuine labor market cracking rather than noise, prompting equity markets to price a higher recession probability and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month; in easing regimes the same combination is interpreted as justified policy accommodation and the effect reverses.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth decelerates while UNRATE simultaneously rises
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE deterioration signal fails to produce significantly more negative 20-day returns than UNRATE alone during tightening regimes, or the direction does not flip sign when tested in an easing-regime subsample.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock housing recovery regime, sustained increases in HOUST from depressed levels signal renewed construction activity and household confidence that compounds through consumer durables spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; in a late-cycle overheating regime the same rise instead signals capacity strain and produces no reliable positive effect.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST growth and 60-day forward returns is not meaningfully higher (by at least 0.03) in recovery-regime subsamples compared to late-cycle subsamples, indicating the regime conditionality does not hold.

mechanism_narrative: In a low nominal-rate environment (FEDFUNDS near 0.08%) combined with elevated CPIAUCSL, the implied real policy rate is deeply negative, which historically fuels equity valuation expansion via cheap real financing costs and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter; this mechanism requires the joint condition since low nominal rates alone during disinflation do not produce the same effect.
candidate_trigger: implied real rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL YoY inflation) below -2%
failure_condition: The joint negative-real-rate trigger fails to produce 60-day forward returns significantly above the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or the FEDFUNDS-only baseline achieves equal or higher Rank IC, indicating no genuine interaction benefit.
